verdict_reasoning: ITPR1 (inositol 1,4,5-trisphosphate receptor type 1) is a large intracellular Ca2+ release channel residing primarily on the endoplasmic reticulum membrane. Its channel pore and all functional domains face the ER lumen or cytoplasm; there is no documented stable pool at the plasma membrane outer leaflet. Checking contextual buckets: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented stress/activation-induced PM surface display; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no lineage-specific PM expression reported; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — ITPR1 is ER-resident, not lysosomal; (4) dual_localization — occasional reports of PM-localized IP3R channels exist in specialized contexts but functional evidence points to cytoplasmic face only, not extracellular accessibility; (5) stable_surface_attachment — no secreted form or wash-resistant PM anchoring. No antibody/ADC/CAR-T programs target extracellular ITPR1. The protein is an endomembrane (ER) resident channel with all ligand-binding and regulatory domains on the cytoplasmic face.
